rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERWIN FONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/16/2021 to allow a court investigator's report to be completed. 1) Paragraph 4b of the petition refers to the Court's order dated 04/12/2010, but the Court's register of actions does not show any order issued on that date. 2) Exhibit A only describes the petitioner's services from 2010 through 2012. 3) Please provide the petitioner's time records for her services as conservator of the estate during this account period. 4) Paragraph 9 requests renewed authority for fees on account, but neither the petition caption nor the notice of hearing specified this relief. It should be noted that the 04/21/2004 order only allowed an hourly rate of $90 for the services enumerated in the petition filed on 03/10/2004. No fee schedules appear to have been filed under Probate Code sections 2614.7, 2614.8 & 2643.1(a). 5) Paragraph 10 of the petition refers to Exhibit B, but there is no Exhibit B attached to the petition. 6) "Account Current" box is not checked on the summary of account. 7) The bond premiums appear to remain the same since 2015, despite the bond reductions approved in the prior accounts. Would the $37,000 additional reduction requested by this petition save any costs?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GWENDOLYN NELICE MCGRAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/02/2021. Issues: 1) Please explain/provide evidence if the original Will has been lodged with the court. (Probate Code 8200). 2) Please file a copy of the AXA Life Insurance Policy showing the beneficiaries. 3) Notice of hearing shows notice being given to Tyrone McGraw on 12/09/2020 who's death certificate shows date of death of 03/17/2020. Please give notice to Mya McGraw and Tiara McGraw. (Probate Code 1202). 4) Sharon L. McGraw has not filed consent for beneficiary to receive conservatorship estate tangible personal property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/16/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY JEAN MCFADDEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/21. (No more hearing spots or earlier date available.) The administrator did not seek prior approval to pay litigation fees. [LRSF 14.51.] There does not appear to be any requirement in the settlement agreements for Court approval. If Court approval is sought, notwithstanding Probate Code Section 10553, notice must be given to all parties in the settlement. A declaration by the attorney or the fiduciary (whoever will retain the original bank statements) must be filed and must include the language required by LRSF 2.11 M.4 regarding the period of time for retaining originals. Some of the attorney's costs need further clarification, especially as no dates for any of the transactions were set forth. For example, costs for certified copies or E-filing do not indicate what was copied or E-filed, and the amounts are not consistent with the Court's records. The case number for the unlawful detainer action against Tiffany Salcedo is incorrect. The Summary of Account on page 4 of the petition does not balance. The administrator may also be asked to explain why there were so many and frequent house and garage cleaning expenses, totaling $4,650 paid to individuals alone. Two payments on of $200 6/5/2020 appear to be duplicates or to a couple?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/2021. Issues: 1) Please explain why $1,700,000.00 bond is sufficient when the I&A alleges $1,898,062.86 in assets. LRSF 14.16 Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond. See CRC 7.204. When the bond of a fiduciary must be increased, the Court favors filing of an additional bond rather than a substitute bond.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD ROBERT ADLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the disclosure re creditors' claims did not meet the requirements of CRC 7.403 (and not all claims were actually "satisfied in full" but for compromised lesser amounts), there was nothing in the report regarding actions the administrator took under her IAEA authority, as required by CRC 7.550, the statutory fee calculation was incorrect (which, however, will not affect the attorney's acceptance of a lower fee), and the filing fee for this final petition was $435, not $450. Item 4 of an I&A should not be completed when a Probate Referee was appointed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL AQUINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. Issues: (matter may be taken off calendar at the hearing for failure to respond) 1) Matter set for hearing for failure to respond to the following Examiner notes: a. Petitioner did not select a box on page 1 under 1 and did not specify the name of the newspaper. b. On page 1 #2 under name of Petitioner is blank. c. Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. d. On page 1 under #2 c. petitioner did not check a box full or limited authority. e. On page 1 under #2 d. Petitioner selected bond not be required for the reasons stated in item 3e and then under 3e. petitioner did not select a box. f. On page 1 under #3.a Petitioner left place of death blank. g. On page 2 under 3. D. Petitioner states the personal property is $2,700,000.00 and it appears Petitioner alleges the annual gross income from real property is $6,000.00. Please explain why the subtotal adds up to $2,707,200.00. Please explain why gross fair market value of real property is blank if there is $6,000.00 in annual gross income from real property. Please explain why net value of real property is blank. h. Petitioner selected Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary and then selected on page 2 under #3 f. Decedent died intestate. Please explain. i. On page 2 under #3h petitioner did not select boxes 1 resident of California or 2 nonresident of California and 3 resident of United States or 4 nonresident of United States have not been selected. j. Please explain why Petitioner did not select a box on page 3 under #5 a (1) spouse or (2) no spouse) when there is a spouse listed on page 4 under #8. k. Please explain why Petitioner did not include an Attachment 3(f)(3) that includes a statement of the testamentary words or their substance and states reasons in that attachment why the presumption in Probate Code 6124 does not apply. l. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). m. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). n. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. o. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. d. On page 1 under #2 c. petitioner did not check a box full or limited authority. e. On page 1 under #2 d. Petitioner selected bond not be required for the reasons stated in item 3e and then under 3e. petitioner did not select a box. f. On page 1 under #3.a Petitioner left place of death blank. g. On page 2 under 3. D. Petitioner states the personal property is $2,700,000.00 and it appears Petitioner alleges the annual gross income from real property is $6,000.00. Please explain why the subtotal adds up to $2,707,200.00. Please explain why gross fair market value of real property is blank if there is $6,000.00 in annual gross income from real property. Please explain why net value of real property is blank. h. Petitioner selected Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary and then selected on page 2 under #3 f. Decedent died intestate. Please explain. i. On page 2 under #3h petitioner did not select boxes 1 resident of California or 2 nonresident of California and 3 resident of United States or 4 nonresident of United States have not been selected. j. Please explain why Petitioner did not select a box on page 3 under #5 a (1) spouse or (2) no spouse) when there is a spouse listed on page 4 under #8. k. Please explain why Petitioner did not include an Attachment 3(f)(3) that includes a statement of the testamentary words or their substance and states reasons in that attachment why the presumption in Probate Code 6124 does not apply. l. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). m. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). n. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. o. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

[LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The proof of service filed on 1/21/21 was the wrong form of notice for an 850 petition. A courtesy copy of the petition and any supporting documents, and the proof of service of the correct Notice of Hearing must be received by the Examiner by no later than 2/26/21.

rulings.Case Title: **WILL TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY** THE ESTATE OF JOSEFINA G. DIZON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/23/21. Although no courtesy copy of the 'Response' filed on 2/1/21 has been received yet by the Examiner, it was skimmed online in the interest of time. After the case is opened in the proper venue, the petitioner may file an ex parte petition to transfer the original will to the recipient court, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8203. If the estate cannot be opened at least 10 days prior to 3/23/21, Petitioner should contact the Examiner to request an additional continuance.
